taxonomy: Anurus nissolia is classified as a species of frog. It belongs to the family Microhylidae, commonly known as the narrow-mouthed toads. The genus Anurus is a monotypic group, containing only the species A. nissolia. This classification places A. nissolia within the order Anura, which includes all frogs. The exact classification may be subject to revision based on ongoing research and taxonomic studies.
uses: Anurus nissolia is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts are said to have antioxidant effects, which can protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In traditional medicine, Anurus nissolia is often used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.
